seo.title: LTX 2.3 Fast Specs, Pricing & Max Length | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: LTX 2.3 Fast specs, pricing and max length. Compare 6–20s outputs, 1080p to 4K, 16:9 and 9:16, native audio, and first/last-frame control.

overview: LTX 2.3 Fast in MaxVideoAI focuses on fast text-to-video and image-to-video generation, including native audio, start/end image transitions, 16:9 or 9:16 output, and longer 6 to 20 second clips.

hero.title: LTX 2.3 Fast - fast generate-first LTX workflows
hero.intro: Use LTX 2.3 Fast when you want lower-cost, faster text-to-video or image-to-video runs with audio, portrait support, and longer 1080p tests before moving winners to Pro.

secondaryLinks.label: Compare LTX 2.3 Fast vs LTX 2.3 Pro ->

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: 6 s to 20 s for Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video

technicalOverview.label: Long-duration constraint
technicalOverview.body: Durations above 10 s require 1080p at 25 fps in the current public routing

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text prompts or a start image, with an optional end image for Image-to-Video transitions

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio toggle for generate modes

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: Pay-as-you-go; displayed generate pricing starts around $0.05/s at 1080p, $0.10/s at 1440p, and $0.21/s at 4K

promptStructure.title: Prompting LTX 2.3 Fast
promptStructure.quote: Subject: [who/what]. Action: [single beat]. Camera: [move]. Look: [lighting + color]. Duration: [6-20s]. Format: [16:9 or 9:16]. Audio: [ambience, music or silence].
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts tighter than you would for a premium final. LTX 2.3 Fast is best when you define one clear motion idea, one camera move, and one visual mood.
steps: Lead with one subject and one action.
Describe the camera move before secondary styling.
Specify 16:9 or 9:16 based on the destination format.
Use 6 to 10 seconds for the broadest quality/fps/resolution flexibility.
When using Image-to-Video, let the start image carry identity and use the prompt for motion.

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use LTX 2.3 Fast for idea volume, then rerun winning concepts in LTX 2.3 Pro when you need more workflow flexibility.
For 12 to 20 seconds, accept the 1080p and 25 fps constraint instead of fighting it.
Use an end image only when the destination frame matters for the cut.
Audio is available here, but Audio-to-Video, Extend and Retake are not.

compareLink.label: Compare LTX 2.3 Fast vs LTX 2.3 Pro ->

prompts.title: Fast social product hook
prompts.prompt: A 9:16 beauty shot of a skincare bottle on a wet mirror surface, quick push-in, clean reflections, soft high-key lighting, 6 seconds, crisp sonic accent at the end.

prompts.title: Image-to-Video start/end transition
prompts.prompt: Animate the uploaded start image of a parked sports car into a smooth evening city pull-away, then land on the uploaded end image as taillights streak across the frame.

prompts.title: Longer draft beat
prompts.prompt: A 16:9 office walkthrough for an internal explainer teaser, one smooth camera move through a bright workspace, realistic motion, light ambience, 14 seconds at 1080p.

faqs.q: Does LTX 2.3 Fast support Audio-to-Video, Extend or Retake?
faqs.a: No. In the current public Fal routing, Fast covers only text-to-video and image-to-video.

faqs.q: Can LTX 2.3 Fast generate vertical video?
faqs.a: Yes. The standard generate workflows support both 16:9 and 9:16.
Fast can still render up to 20 seconds, but runs above 10 seconds are constrained to 1080p at 25 fps.

faqs.q: When should I move from Fast to LTX 2.3 Pro?
faqs.a: Stay on Fast while testing hooks, pacing and format variants. Move to LTX 2.3 Pro when you need Audio-to-Video, Extend, Retake, or a more complete end-to-end LTX workflow.

custom.heroDesc1: LTX 2.3 Fast is the generate-first version of the new LTX 2.3 family. It keeps the broad text/image generation surface, native audio and start/end image support, but drops the editing workflows that belong to LTX 2.3 Pro.
custom.heroDesc2: That makes it a cleaner fit for rapid concepting, social aspect-ratio variants and quick hook testing, especially when you want more clip length than ultra-short engines usually allow.
custom.heroSupportLine: Use Fast for speed and volume, then promote only the winners to LTX 2.3 Pro if the project needs deeper control.
heroHighlights: Fast iteration path||Good for testing multiple prompt angles before committing to a premium pass.
Longer clips than many fast engines||You can push from 6 to 20 seconds when the pacing needs more room.
Still includes native audio||Useful for rough timing, mood and social-first sound design.
Clean split versus standard||You know exactly what Fast is for: generate quickly, not extend or retake.
custom.specTitle: Real Specs - LTX 2.3 Fast in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: Specs reflect the live public Fast routing exposed today.

specSections.title: Fast generate workflows
specSections.intro: Fast is optimized around Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video only. It is the easiest LTX 2.3 entry point when the goal is to test more ideas, faster.
items: Use Image-to-Video when the first frame or product identity matters.
Keep the prompt focused on one motion beat.

specSections.title: What Fast does not include
specSections.intro: The product boundary is important for clear UX. Audio-to-Video, Extend and Retake stay on LTX 2.3 Pro so Fast remains simpler and more predictable.
items: Move up to standard when those controls matter.

custom.whatTitle: How LTX 2.3 Fast works in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Pick LTX 2.3 Fast, stay in the standard generate flow, then switch between text-driven and image-driven input by adding or removing the start image.
custom.whatIntro2: The page intentionally avoids standard-LTX editing modes so the options stay coherent with the public Fast routing.
whatFlowSteps: Pick LTX 2.3 Fast as the engine.
Use text only for Text-to-Video or add a start image for Image-to-Video.
Optionally add an end image to define the last frame of the transition.
Choose duration, aspect ratio, resolution and fps, keeping the >10 s constraint in mind.
Check the live price and use Fast for broad iteration before upgrading winners.
custom.galleryIntro: Local placeholders are used for now on this dev branch. Replace them with final approved LTX 2.3 Fast renders before production launch.
strengths: Good cost/speed balance for concept exploration, hook testing and aspect-ratio variants.
Useful when you want a lighter-weight LTX page without extend/retake complexity.
Start/end image support makes it practical for simple transformation cuts and reveal transitions.
boundaries: No Audio-to-Video, Extend or Retake on the Fast route.
Durations above 10 seconds are constrained to 1080p at 25 fps.
Fast is best used for generation, not source-video repair workflows.
safetyRules: Do not generate sexual content or anything involving minors.
Do not impersonate real people or public figures without authorization.
Do not upload private or sensitive personal data.
Keep prompts brand-safe and rights-cleared when audio is involved.
demoPrompt: A vertical beauty ad opener, close-up on a product as condensation slides across the bottle, fast push-in, crisp reflections, subtle impact sound, 6 seconds.
demoNotes: Clear first shot and sound cue
Easy to rerun in multiple variants
custom.multishotIntro1: Fast can run longer than the LTX 2.3 Pro generate path, which is useful for simple walkthroughs and pacing tests.
custom.multishotIntro2: Just remember that >10 second runs trade some flexibility for routing stability.
multishotTips: Use 6 to 10 seconds for the highest flexibility.
Use 12 to 20 seconds only when the extra room is worth the 1080p/25 fps constraint.
custom.imageIntro: Fast still supports a start image plus an optional end image, which makes it useful for simple transition planning without moving to a heavier workflow.
imageFlow: Upload the start image that should anchor the first frame.
Optionally upload an end image when the last frame matters.
Use the prompt to define the motion and pacing between those two states.
imageWhy: Helpful for ad cutdowns and social transition tests
comparisonPoints: Choose Fast for idea volume, social variants and first-pass motion tests.
Move to LTX 2.3 Pro when you need Audio-to-Video, Extend or Retake.
Use Fast when the job is generation-only and simplicity is more important than editing controls.
custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare LTX 2.3 Fast with LTX 2.3, Veo Fast and older LTX fast tiers while keeping the same page structure.
custom.finalPara1: Ready for a lighter LTX generation workflow? Open LTX 2.3 Fast in Generate.
custom.finalPara2: Use Fast for speed, then move the winning prompt to LTX 2.3 Pro if the project needs deeper editing tools.
custom.promptingTitle: How to Write a Great LTX 2.3 Fast Prompt
custom.promptingIntro: LTX 2.3 Fast is a generate-first engine, so keep the prompt short, visual and direct.
custom.promptingTip: Tip: the UI already handles duration, aspect ratio, resolution, fps and the long-duration routing constraint. Focus the prompt on one subject, one move and one mood.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: One subject, one action and one move beat longer wish-list prompts.
Use start images for identity and prompts for motion.
Reserve 12 to 20 second runs for cases where the extra pacing room really matters.
promptingEngineWhy: Fast responds well to concise prompts.
Image-to-Video works best when the still already contains the desired composition.
Start/end image transitions are most reliable when the two frames are clearly related.

promptingTabNotes.quick: Quick = fastest path to usable drafts.
promptingTabNotes.structured: Structured = best when multiple teammates need to reuse the prompt.
promptingTabNotes.pro: Pro = use only when you need stronger continuity inside a generate-only flow.
promptingTabNotes.storyboard: Storyboard = helpful for simple multi-beat social sequences.

promptingTabs.label: Quick
promptingTabs.title: Quick prompt (fastest draft)
promptingTabs.description: Use this for rapid variants and hook testing.
promptingTabs.copy: [Subject + one action], [camera move], [lighting/style], [optional sound cue], [16:9 or 9:16 vibe].

promptingTabs.label: Structured
promptingTabs.title: Structured prompt (repeatable)
promptingTabs.description: Use labeled sections when several people will reuse the same prompt logic.
promptingTabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what]

Action:
[One clean motion beat]

Camera:
[Shot + one move]

Look:
[Lighting + palette]

Audio:
[Ambience/music cue if needed]

promptingTabs.label: Image flow
promptingTabs.title: Image-to-Video prompt
promptingTabs.description: Best when the still frame should stay recognizable.
promptingTabs.copy:
Start frame:
[What the uploaded image already shows]

Motion:
[What should move]

Camera:
[Push / pan / drift / orbit]

Resolve:
[Optional end-image target]

promptingTabs.label: Storyboard
promptingTabs.title: Storyboard prompt (generate-only sequence)
promptingTabs.description: Use this for short multi-beat clips without switching workflows.
promptingTabs.copy:
Shot 1: [setup]
Shot 2: [core action]
Shot 3: [resolve]

Look:
[Lighting + grade]

Audio:
[Ambience + one cue]

troubleshootingItems: Longer runs look less flexible -> keep >10 second clips only for cases where the extra length is worth the 1080p/25 fps cap.
Image-to-Video feels weak -> improve the start image composition before adding more prompt detail.
Start-to-end transition feels forced -> make the end image closer to the same visual world.
The clip feels too busy -> reduce to one action and one camera move.
You need source-video editing -> move to LTX 2.3 Pro instead of overloading Fast.
